narrative:
Apple unveiled new MacBook Air and MacBook Pro models with M5 chips on Tuesday, March 3, 2026, marking the company's biggest Mac refresh in over a year [2]. However, the updates come with significant price increases across all models, driven by a global RAM shortage affecting the tech industry [4].

## Price Increases and Storage Upgrades

The MacBook Air M5 now starts at $1,099 for the 13-inch model and $1,299 for the 15-inch, representing $100 increases from their M4 predecessors [1][2][7]. The MacBook Pro sees even steeper price hikes, with the 14-inch M5 Pro starting at $2,199 (up from $1,999) and the 16-inch M5 Max at $3,899 (up $400 from its predecessor) [2][4]. Apple justified these increases by doubling base storage across the lineup—MacBook Air models now start with 512GB instead of 256GB, while MacBook Pro M5 Pro models begin at 1TB and M5 Max models at 2TB [1][2][5].

## New M5 Pro and M5 Max Architecture

The M5 Pro and M5 Max represent a significant departure from previous Apple Silicon designs, featuring what Apple calls a "Fusion Architecture" that combines two silicon chiplets into a single processor [3][6][8]. This marks the first time Apple has used this multi-die approach for Pro and Max chips, previously reserved only for Ultra configurations [3]. Both chips feature an 18-core CPU with six "super cores" (Apple's new branding for high-performance cores) and 12 new performance cores optimized for multithreaded workloads [3][5][6][8].

## Performance Claims and AI Focus

Apple positions the M5 chips as delivering substantial AI performance improvements, claiming the M5 Pro and M5 Max offer "over 4x the peak GPU compute for AI compared to the previous generation" [6][8]. For the MacBook Air M5, Apple claims 4x faster AI performance compared to the M4 model [7]. The company also states that MacBook Pro users will see large language model prompts processed "nearly four times faster than comparable M4-based machines" [2]. Graphics performance is said to improve by up to 20% overall, with ray-tracing workloads seeing up to 35% improvements [5][6].

## Market Context and Industry Impact

The price increases come amid a global RAM shortage caused by high demand from AI data centers, which analysts predict will impact smartphone shipments and other consumer hardware [4]. Apple's Mac business has struggled recently, with sales dropping nearly 7% to $8.39 billion during the holiday quarter, falling short of analyst expectations of nearly $9 billion [2]. The new machines are positioned to encourage upgrades, particularly from users with older Intel-era systems or early M-series devices [2].

## Additional Features and Availability

All new MacBook models include Apple's N1 wireless chip, enabling Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 6 connectivity [1][5][7]. The MacBook Air M5 maintains the same battery life as its predecessor, while the MacBook Pro is rated for up to 24 hours [1][9]. Pre-orders begin March 4 at 9:15 AM ET, with availability starting March 11 [7][9]. Apple also updated its display lineup, introducing a new two-tier Studio Display family to replace the aging Pro Display XDR [2].

citations.title: MacBook Air M5 vs. MacBook Air M4: What's changed beyond the Apple silicon
citations.quote: Apple unveiled a new MacBook Air today, and apart from the new M5 chip, things don't look remarkably different. Sure, it's getting a mild refresh, but maybe not in the way most people would want. Namely, it's more expensive — a $100 price bump across all models.

citations.title: Apple raises MacBook prices across the board as M5 chips, new displays signal AI-first strategy
citations.quote: Apple on Tuesday rolled out new MacBook Pro and MacBook Air models with its latest M5 chips, along with an updated Studio Display lineup, in its biggest Mac refresh in more than a year.

citations.title: M5 Pro and M5 Max are surprisingly big departures from older Apple Silicon
citations.quote: Apple says that M5 Pro and M5 Max use an 'all-new Fusion Architecture' that welds two silicon chiplets into a single processor. Apple has used this approach before, but historically only to combine two Max chips together into an Ultra.

citations.title: The new MacBook Pro laptops are as much as $400 more expensive than their predecessors. Thank the RAM shortage.
citations.quote: With the demands for more computers and data centers to power AI, the market is experiencing a shortage of RAM, causing memory prices to surge.

citations.title: Apple intros M5 Pro and Max MacBook Pros and its first new monitors in years
citations.quote: Both use an 18-core CPU with six higher-performing cores and 12 lower-performing cores, but Apple is changing how it talks about each kind of core. The high-performance cores are now called 'super cores'

citations.title: Apple unveils M5 Pro and M5 Max chips with new 'Fusion Architecture'
citations.quote: The tech giant says the chips are engineered around its new Fusion Architecture, an advanced design that merges two dies into a single, high-performance system on a chip (SoC)

citations.title: The MacBook Air M5 starts at $1,099, up $100 from the M4
citations.quote: Apple is reverting a price cut from last year. It dropped the M4-powered MBA's starting price down to $999, but for the M5-equipped model, you'll need to shell out at least $1,099.

citations.title: Apple unveils the M5 Pro and M5 Max chips, which feature new, faster 'super cores'
citations.quote: At the heart of the M5 Pro and M5 Max are what Apple is calling a new 'Fusion Architecture' that 'combines two dies into a single system on a chip (SoC).'

citations.title: Apple brings its M5 Pro and Max silicon to the next MacBook Pro generation
citations.quote: Pre-orders will open on March 4 with an expected ship date of March 11. You'll want to save some extra coin this time, though, as prices have gone up compared to the prior generation.

keyDevelopments: Apple announces M5 MacBook Air and MacBook Pro with significant price increases across all models
Introduction of new 'Fusion Architecture' using dual chiplets in M5 Pro and M5 Max chips
Base storage doubled across MacBook lineup (Air: 256GB→512GB, Pro: varies by model)
New three-tier CPU core hierarchy: 'super cores,' performance cores, and efficiency cores
Major AI performance claims: 4x faster AI compute compared to previous generation
Global RAM shortage cited as driver for price increases
Apple's N1 wireless chip enables Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 6 across new MacBook lineup
